_comment: Twin Cobra (Toaplan) I/O map. Authoritative source: MAME src/mame/toaplan/twincobr.cpp address_maps (verified verbatim, not from memory). THREE CPUs: 68000 main (near-native on Amiga), Z80 sound, TMS32010 DSP. The DSP and Z80 each share a RAM window with the 68000 -- those windows are the protection/comms handshake and must be modelled exactly. NOTE: the tile layers are NOT directly memory-mapped: you load a VRAM address via the CRTC (0x060001/3) then read/write through the data ports at 0x07e00x (Toaplan address-then-data quirk).

m68000._role: main game logic -- runs near-native on the Amiga 68k

0x030000-0x033fff.comment: MAIN WORK RAM, ALSO the 68K<->TMS32010 shared window (DSP reads/writes it via its ports 00/01)

0x07e000.comment: text VRAM data port (addr set via CRTC 0x060001/3)

0x060003.comment: CRTC data register (also selects VRAM access address)

0x070000.comment: text layer scroll (x at 0x070000/1, y at 0x070002/3)

0x07800d.comment: Twin Cobra DSP/system control latch -- enables/triggers the DSP, screen flip, etc. KEY protection handshake.

z80._role: sound driver (YM3812/OPL2); small -- interpret with vendored z80.c or sample-stub

0x10.comment: Twin Cobra coin/start inputs (read on some sets)

tms32010_dsp._role: PROTECTION + scroll/sprite math. Make-or-break. No core vendored yet; MAME tms32010 is the reference.

0x000-0x7ff.comment: dsp_21.bin + dsp_22.bin -> 16-bit opcodes, word access only

port0.comment: address of 68K memory the DSP wants to access

port1.comment: data read/written at the 68K address in port0 -> reaches into 68K 0x030000-0x033fff

port3_bit15.comment: drives the DSP BIO branch input

handshakes.m68k_dsp: 68K shares 0x030000-0x033fff (its work RAM). DSP reaches in via ports 00 (addr) / 01 (data). 68K controls the DSP through the 0x07800d system latch. Game hangs / mis-scrolls if this is wrong.
handshakes.m68k_z80: 68K window 0x07a000-0x07afff == Z80 0x8000-0x87ff. Command/response bytes for sound.
